REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS SOON DUCK LEE AND YOUNG IL LEE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. NO EVIDENCE PROVIDED IN THE MOVING PAPERS TO SUPPORT DEFENDANTS' CLAIM THAT THE DEFAULT WAS ENTERED DUE TO EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. DECLARATION PROVIDED IN REPLY IS CONCLUSORY AND DOES NOT ESTABLISH EXCUSABLE NEGLECT RE: FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE PROPERLY SERVED SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, E.G. (1) DEFENDANTS DID NOT UNDERSTAND INSTRUCTIONS ON THE SUMMONS "YOU HAVE 30 CALENDAR DAYS TO FILE A RESPONSE; " (2) WHAT COVID-19 MITIGATION MEASURES TOOK DEFENDANT'S ATTENTION AWAY FROM RESPONDING TO THE COMPLAINT BETWEEN DECEMBER 21, 2020 AND JANUARY 21, 2021, ETC. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THE PROPOSED ANSWER ATTACHED TO THE MOVING PAPERS DOES NOT MEET THE BASIC PLEADING REQUIREMENTS RE: AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jun-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:(Zoom)
Aleksandr Volkov, 415.987.7000 for Plaintiff Peter Kiritchenko.
Arthur Lipton, 415.362.6812 for Defendants Soon Duck Lee and Young Lee.
Defendant Soon Duck Lee is present.

The Court addresses representations by the Defendant that the Court was closed due to the shelter in place. The Court states that during the shelter in place that there were no Court closures.

Mr. Lipton states that when the Defendant went to Courthouse she couldn't get help due to the Shelter in place.

The Court states that although English is the Defendant's second language the Defendant has to be diligent. The Court finds that there is no sufficient show of diligence. The request for sanctions and attorney fees are Denied. Mr. Volkov states that he included the request for attorney's fees if the motion was granted.

Argued. The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANTS SOON LEE and YOUNG LEE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is Denied. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Garrett L. Wong; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2021 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Motion For Prove Up Hearing In Support Of Application For Default Judgment Against Defendants Soon Duck Lee And Young Il Lee
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Motion for Entry of Default Judgment as to defendants Soon Duck Lee and Young Il Lee.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Aleksandr A Volkov, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs Peter Kiritchenko and Oksana Kiritchenko, who were placed under oath and testified.
Messrs. Volkov, Kiritchenko and Ms. Kiritchenko appeared via Zoom for this morning's proceedings.
Linda Huang, CPA/Bookeeper and witness for plaintiffs was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Huang appeared telephonically for this morning's proceedings.
Carl Michelsen, Soil Engineer and witness for plaintiffs, was placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Michelsen appeared via Zoom for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants Soon Duck Lee and Young Il Lee.

Plaintiffs Complaint for Breach of Contract and Negligence, was filed on November 13, 2020.
Personally served on defendant Soon Duck Lee on December 9, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiffs complaint on defendant Young Il Lee occurred on December 11, 2020, and effectuated on December 21, 2020.
Default as to both defendants Soon Duck Lee and Young Il Lee was entered on January 21, 2021.
Dismissal without prejudice as to DOE defendants was filed on February 10, 2021.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Peter Kiritchenko and Oksana Kiritchenko, and against the defaulted defendants Soon Duck Lee and Young Il Lee as follows:

Principal - $150,000.00
Attorney Fees - $2,032.08
Costs - $622.50
Total - $152,654.58.

The proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Arthur Lipton; Lipton & Piper LLP; 415-362-6286; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Soon Duck Lee and Young Il Lee.
Aleksandr Volkov; Volkov Law Office; 415-987-7000; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs Peter Kiritchenko and Oksana Kiritchenko.

Defendants contest the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS SOON DUCK LEE and YOUNG IL LEE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is DENIED without prejudice. No evidence provided in the moving papers to support defendants' claim that the default was entered due to excusable neglect. Declaration provided in reply is conclusory and does not establish excusable neglect re: failure to respond to the properly served summons and complaint, e.g. (1) Defendants did not understand instructions on the summons "You have 30 calendar days to file a response; " (2) what COVID-19 mitigation measures took defendant's attention away from responding to the complaint between December 21, 2020 and January 21, 2021, etc. The Court further notes that the proposed answer attached to the moving papers does not meet the basic pleading requirements re: affirmative defenses. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
